question_text: A student drops a tennis ball from a height of 5 metres on Earth (g = 10 N/kg) and another identical ball from the same height on Jupiter (g = 25 N/kg). Ignoring air resistance, which statement is correct?

options.label: A
options.text: Both balls hit the ground with the same speed because they fall from the same height

options.label: B
options.text: The Jupiter ball hits faster because the stronger gravity accelerates it more

options.label: C
options.text: The Earth ball hits faster because Earth's gravity is stronger overall

options.label: D
options.text: The balls hit at the same time but with different speeds

correct_answer: The Jupiter ball hits faster because the stronger gravity accelerates it more
explanation: Using v² = 2gh: on Earth, v² = 2 × 10 × 5 = 100, so v = 10 m/s. On Jupiter, v² = 2 × 25 × 5 = 250, so v ≈ 15.8 m/s. The stronger gravitational field on Jupiter causes greater acceleration, resulting in higher impact speed. Both balls experience greater gravitational force proportional to their mass.
